issues.description: Feature implementation promotion still depends on upstream evidence and test statuses that can be inferred too loosely. For runtime-facing features, implementation_status implemented must require executed behavioral proof rather than structural proof-chain closure alone.
issues.title: implemented feature status still depends on inferred closure

issues.description: Define governed repo-local policy that separates contract represented, artifact present, executed behavior, and implementation truth. Specify which statuses are operator-declarable versus derived-only, and require execution-backed behavioral proof for runtime-facing implemented features.
issues.title: Govern runtime-backed proof for implementation status

issues.description: registry sync-statuses currently promotes evidence toward passed from artifact presence and tier/link alignment rather than executed verification provenance. Distinguish artifact collected from behavior proven, and prevent presence-only promotion to passed.
issues.title: status sync overstates passed evidence from artifact presence

issues.description: registry sync-statuses currently promotes tests toward passing from test-path existence and linked evidence status rather than executed test results. Require execution-backed proof for passing and prevent structural inference from replacing runtime test truth.
issues.title: status sync overstates passing tests from path and linked evidence
